items.title: May 24, 1991: A James Worthy breakaway dunk off the underhand lead pass from Magic Johnson during the Lakers' 106-92 Game 3 win over the Portland Trail Blazers in the Western Conference Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
May 24, 1991: A James Worthy breakaway dunk off the underhand lead pass from Magic Johnson during the Lakers' 106-92 Game 3 win over the Portland Trail Blazers in the Western Conference Finals.

Worthy scored 25 PTS. Magic had 14 PTS/19 AST as Los Angeles took a 2-1 series lead.

items.title: May 24, 1991: Clyde Drexler drives past Byron Scott and finishes with a lefty dunk in Game 3 of the Western Conference Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
May 24, 1991: Clyde Drexler drives past Byron Scott and finishes with a lefty dunk in Game 3 of the Western Conference Finals.

Drexler had 18 PTS/8 REB/6 AST for Portland but the Lakers defeated the Blazers, 106-92, to take a 2-1 series lead.

items.title: May 23, 1990: In the final 30 seconds, Portland's Terry Porter hits a shot to tie, then a shot to win Game 2 of the Western Conference Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
May 23, 1990: In the final 30 seconds, Portland's Terry Porter hits a shot to tie, then a shot to win Game 2 of the Western Conference Finals.

Porter scored 28 PTS and Jerome Kersey had 29 PTS/11 REB as the Blazers took a 2-0 series lead with a 108-107 win over the Phoenix Suns.

items.title: May 23, 1984: Houston wins a coin flip over Portland, and will draft first in the upcoming NBA Draft.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
May 23, 1984: Houston wins a coin flip over Portland, and will draft first in the upcoming NBA Draft.

Rockets head coach Bill Fitch says they will use the #1 pick to select Akeem Olajuwon. Blazers owner Larry Weinberg said his team is "very interested" in taking Sam Bowie at #2.

items.title: Hawks' Dyson Daniels made second team All-Defense. NAW somewhat surprisingly did not get a single vote.
items.author: John Hollinger
items.body_excerpt:
Hawks' Dyson Daniels made second team All-Defense. NAW somewhat surprisingly did not get a single vote.

I'm a bit surprised Portland's Toumani Camara didn't get more love, after Blazers made playoffs and he drew an unfathomable number of offensive fouls, but overall a defensible vote.

items.title: May 22, 1977: Philadelphia’s Julius Erving dunks on Portland’s Bill Walton during Game 1 of the NBA Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
May 22, 1977: Philadelphia’s Julius Erving dunks on Portland’s Bill Walton during Game 1 of the NBA Finals.

Dr. J scored a game-high 33 PTS (14-24 FG) and Doug Collins had 30 PTS/6 AST/6 REB in the 76ers’ 107-101 win. Walton led the Trail Blazers with 28 PTS (11-17 FG)/20 REB.
